rulings.Case Title: PAMELA SNYDER VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctins, Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pamela Snyder
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 13, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctins, Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pamela Snyder. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, Terminating Sanctions, and For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff is granted in part and denied in part without prejudice. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions against Plaintiff is awarded in the amount of $1,665.00 as required by the Court's 9/9/19 order. The supplemental declaration requested by the Court's 9/9/19 order does not request or provide evidence supporting a different award. It also does not explain how the discovery it attaches supports any particular evidentiary or issue sanction, and the Court finds that such sanctions are not warranted at this time. However, by failing to respond timely to Defendant's written discovery requests, Plaintiff has waived any objections to the discovery requests and must either respond to each request in full or file a motion for reconsideration of the Court's October 16, 2018 Order and seek a protective order, no later than October 4, 2019, subject to agreement by the parties to provide Plaintiff additional time to respond to some or all of the requests. If Plaintiff fails to respond, Defendant may seek additional monetary sanctions. Defendant may renew its motion for evidence and issue sanctions if it cannot obtain discovery sought through its form interrogatories and 142 special interrogatories by deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO ET AL VS. 2001 THAI STICK INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 13, 2019 on Line 6, DEFENDANT 2001 THAI STICK, INC., THAI STK, INC. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendants 2001 Thai Stick, Inc. and Thai Stk, Inc.'s motion for default relief is granted under the mandatory provisions of CCP §473(b). Defendants' attorney's affidavit attests that default was entered due to his mistakes in not checking the docket and monitoring the action during talks with plaintiffs, when he knew English was not his client's first language and the client thus might not understand documents served on him. California courts split on whether mandatory §473(b) relief is proper when both lawyer and client err. (Martin Potts & Assoc. v. Corsair (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 432, 442.) But public policy favors deciding cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) Defendants' requests for CCP §473(b) discretionary relief are denied as untimely. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §5:366.) Defendants' counsel shall pay plaintiffs' attorney fees of $2,946. When §473(b) relief is granted based on an attorney affidavit, the attorney must "pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs." (Id.) Plaintiffs' request that relief be conditioned on payment is barred by §473(c)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: ZAVER PATEL VS. ICHHARAMBHAI PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Zaver Patel¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; Production Of Documents In Compliance With The Requests; And For A Privilege Log From Defendants Ichharambhai Patel And Patel Enterprises, Lp
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 13, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ZAVER PATEL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; Production Of Documents In Compliance With The Requests; And For A Privilege Log From Defendants Ichharambhai Patel And Patel Enterprises, LP. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. In addition to the issues and caselaw in the parties briefs, the Court requests that the parties please come prepared to address the following: whether the parties have identified any court other than the Gallio court that has recognized or held that the right of privacy under the California Constitution protects estate planning documents from discovery in civil proceedings such that the Court must employ a balancing test before it can order their production in a case; whether any courts have ordered or affirmed production of estate planning documents in civil proceedings; whether any courts have held that the balancing test favors production of documents that fall within a zone of privacy where the producing party can designate the documents confidential or AEO under a protective order; whether courts have ordered parties to provide privilege logs for documents withheld as privileged on grounds of privilege other than attorney-client privilege or attorney work product. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
